mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.38% on 2016-02-01 indicates near-zero rates
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed baseline returns by at least 2% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, triggering positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained job creation
failure_condition: Positive payroll surprises fail to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals demand destruction and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity risk premium expands.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 447,308 million and UNRATE at 4.9% on 2016-02-01
regime_hint: early-stage demand shock or recession onset
failure_condition: Joint signal triggers produce negative 5-day returns in < 60% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_5d on both proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and margin compression that reduces forward earnings guidance, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: supply-side slowdown or commodity deflation cycle
failure_condition: Deceleration periods produce negative 20-day returns in < 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,100 thousand units annualized while real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains positive, signaling sustained demand and multiplier effects that drive positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through construction and consumer wealth channels.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1,100 thousand units AND GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 1.5% annualized
regime_hint: post-crisis recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in < 58% of triggered instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1%, creating negative real rates that compress bond yields and support equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 238.107 and FEDFUNDS at 0.38% on 2016-02-01
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in < 55% of instances, or the real rate spread fails to show significant correlation with forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) declines month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 5.0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < -50,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% and PAYEMS at 143,560 thousand on 2016-02-01
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recessionary shock
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in < 60% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.0% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.0%, signaling productivity gains without cost-push pressures that expand profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 1.0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 106.8477 and CPIAUCSL at 238.107 on 2016-01-01
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in < 58% of instances, or the margin expansion mechanism fails to show significant predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling window).
